ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, FEES
Off Calendar Mar-10-2014 Continued To Jun-30-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard: PES-13-296424 The Estate of Joan Saphir Alexander and PES-14-297476 In Re: Susan Alexander

Appearances: Caroline Hinshaw for and with Robert Alexander. Jacob Glickman for Susan Alexander (Respondent).

Mr. Glickman stated that his Client feels that she has accounted, that she has provided statements for the credit cards, the checking account and the Neuberger Berman account, and that she has given them everything. Ms. Hinshaw stated that there was personal property in the estate that is unaccounted for, that there is significant property missing, that two $60,000.00 checks were issued and that they need more explanation. Mr. Alexander stated that the statements are not comprehensive and that some of the statements are missing for 2011. The accounting petition in case number 296378 was continued to June 30, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court granted the Petition for Order Compelling Attorney-In-Fact to Account, etc. in case number 297476 (subject to review of the order). Attorney's fees are denied without prejudice. The accounting period is to cover from October 2006 through date of death November 25, 2012. Status on filing of the accounting was set for June 30, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Court Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Allen. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, FEES
Off Calendar Feb-10-2014 Continued To Mar-10-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, FEES
Off Calendar Dec-09-2013 Continued To Feb-10-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jacob Glickman for Administrator. Kevin Solan for Robert Alexander.

The Court reminded Counsel that nothing will be left in the Estate after the attorneys' fees are paid. Mr. Glickman stated that they can do a preliminary distribution to Bonnie Alexander in the amount of $350,000.00 plus interest from November 26, 2013 (Mr. Glickman will prepare an order). The remaining issues in the accounting petition were continued to February 10, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Lillian K. Sing. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, FEES
Off Calendar Nov-18-2013 Continued To Dec-09-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Mar-21-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jacob Glickman for Susan Alexander. Robert Saphir Alexander (Son of Decedent) in Propria Persona.

The Court explained the legal meaning of the term "lost will". Mr. Alexander stated that he is in agreement that the copies of the will that he and his Sister have are a copy of the original will. Mr. Alexander also stated that there are communication issues between him and his Sister with regard to his requests for information/financial records for accounts back to 2007, that his Sister is resistant and non-compliant with regard to providing said documents, and that some of the bank accounts are not listed on the spread sheet he was provided with. In addition, Mr. Alexander provided history, stated that the Sister has not provided information/whereabouts regarding funds ($375,000.00) from sale of the New York property, spoke of the Marital Trust/Neuberger-Berman stock broker account not being mentioned, explained that his Mother appointed his Sister at his suggestion because he resides out of the Country, spoke of his Father's Estate and business, and raised the issue of conflict of interest with Mr. Glickman representing his Sister. Mr. Glickman stated that his Client disputes that she ever did anything wrong, explained that this probate is different than the Father's Estate and the Trust, and stated that his Client has provided statements. The Court stated that it has no authorization to deal with the issues raised with regard to the Father's Estate or the Trust, that the only issue before the Court is that of appointment of the Sister, that the will nominates the Sister to act as Executor and waives bond, explained that the Executor needs to do an inventory of the assets and suggested Mr. Alexander speak to an Attorney. Mr. Glickman agreed to provide information to Mr. Alexander via e-mail. The Court granted the Petition for Probate (subject to review of the order). Bond is waived.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy W. Yee.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Feb-19-2013 Continued To Mar-19-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
